headlines.title: Fear of an Oil Glut May Be Overblown, Top Consultancy Says
headlines.summary: Up to 75% of the previous oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz are expected to return to the market by the end of the year, but significantly lower oil prices aren’t guaranteed for 2027 as the ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions are unlikely to be resolved for good soon, Fereidun Feshar

headlines.title: Germany Weathers Energy Crisis With Growing Dependence on American LNG
headlines.summary: The share of LNG imports of Germany’s total gas supply rose to 12% in the first half of the year, up from 10% a year earlier, despite the shock supply loss from the Middle East due to the closed Strait of Hormuz, according to data by the German regulator. headlines.title: More European Investors Oppose Norway’s Push for Arctic Oil Drilling
headlines.summary: Six more financial institutions from Northwestern Europe have voiced their opposition to plans by Norway to pursue Arctic oil drilling in a bid to boost Europe’s supply security. According to the opponents, energy transition efforts should take priority. The new opponents include

headlines.title: Pakistan Buys Second Spot LNG Cargo as Supply Crunch Persists
headlines.summary: Pakistan has bought its second spot market LNG cargo in as many weeks, in a sign that flows of liquefied gas out of the Persian Gulf have been slow to recover. According to Bloomberg, the country bought the cargo from TotalEnergies at a price of $17.37 per million British thermal

headlines.title: Five Energy Stocks Riding Texas's Data Center Power Boom
headlines.summary: Fly into Abilene, Amarillo or Midland this year and you'll see the buildout before you land: cranes over half-finished turbine halls, miles of freshly trenched pipeline right-of-way, and fenced-off scrubland that a year ago grew nothing but mesquite and cotton. headlines.title: The Era of Cheap U.S. Natural Gas May Be Coming to an End
headlines.summary: U.S. natural gas prices are set to rise through 2035, following a decade of low Henry Hub prices, as the AI data center boom and the expansion of U.S. LNG export infrastructure will underpin strong demand, analysts at Wood Mackenzie say. headlines.title: Starmer Out, Burnham In: What Changes for UK Energy Policy?
headlines.summary: With Kier Starmer stepping down as the United Kingdom’s Prime Minister (PM), it looks increasingly likely that Andy Burnham, who is known as the “King of the North”, will take his place as the leader of the Labour Party and PM in July. headlines.title: Dip in U.S. LNG Imports to EU Spells Trouble for Trade Deal
headlines.summary: For two years, the European Union has been the biggest regional buyer of U.S. liquefied natural gas.
